title: Agent Failure Recovery and Work Preservation
description: When an agent is externally terminated (SIGTERM/exit 143), all uncommitted work is lost, the task is left orphaned as IN_PROGRESS, and downstream tasks can bypass dependency gates. This epic covers: WIP commit on termination, incremental commit instructions in prompts, orphaned task recovery, dependency gate hardening, failed session diff archival, and agent heartbeat monitoring. See opensprint.dev-ax8.2 incident for context.

title: Code Cleanup: Reduce Repetition, Improve Modularity, Expand Test Coverage
description: Epic for codebase-wide cleanup identified during a thorough code review. Goals: (1) eliminate duplicate code, (2) improve modularity by breaking up large files and extracting utilities, (3) standardize patterns (service instantiation, error handling, logging), and (4) expand test coverage for untested services. See individual child tasks for specific scope.

title: Standardize frontend loading state patterns in Redux slices
description:
## Problem

Redux slices use inconsistent patterns for tracking loading states:

- executeSlice.ts has 6+ separate boolean flags: loading, statusLoading, taskDetailLoading, archivedLoading, markDoneLoading, unblockLoading
- Other slices use a single loading boolean
- Some slices track errors per-operation, others use a single error string

This leads to boilerplate in extraReducers (every thunk needs pending/fulfilled/rejected for its specific flag) and makes it easy to forget to reset a flag.

Additionally, some components call the API directly (e.g., ExecutePhase.tsx line ~244 calls api.agents.active(projectId) instead of going through a thunk), creating inconsistency in data flow.

## Scope of Work

1. Create a standardized loading state helper, e.g.:

   type AsyncState = { loading: boolean; error: string | null };
   type AsyncStates<K extends string> = Record<K, AsyncState>;

   function createAsyncHandlers<K extends string>(key: K, builder: ActionReducerMapBuilder<State>) { ... }

2. Refactor executeSlice.ts to use the standardized pattern — replace 6 boolean flags with a typed record
3. Audit other slices (planSlice, evalSlice, deploySlice) for similar improvements
4. Move any direct API calls in components (api.agents.active, api.feedback.get in SourceFeedbackSection) into thunks for consistency
5. Update tests for affected slices

## Files to Modify

- packages/frontend/src/store/slices/executeSlice.ts (primary target)
- packages/frontend/src/store/slices/planSlice.ts (audit)
- packages/frontend/src/store/slices/deploySlice.ts (audit)
- packages/frontend/src/pages/phases/ExecutePhase.tsx (move direct API calls to thunks)
- Corresponding test files

## Acceptance Criteria

- executeSlice uses a consistent loading state pattern
- No direct api.xxx() calls in component files (all go through thunks)
- All existing slice tests pass
- Reduced boilerplate in extraReducers

title: Implement 'Plan Tasks' AI generation flow
description: When a plan has no child implementation tasks (or only the gating task), provide a 'Plan Tasks' action that invokes the Planner agent to decompose the plan markdown into individual beads tasks, all blocked by the plan's gating task. Acceptance criteria: (1) Planner agent is invoked with the plan markdown as context. (2) Generated tasks are created as beads child issues under the plan's epic. (3) Each generated task has a 'blocks' dependency on the gating task. (4) Task generation results are reflected in the UI in real time via WebSocket updates.

title: Replying to feedback
Cascade resolve status from parent feedback to child replies
description:
When a user marks a parent feedback item as 'Resolved' on the Evaluate page, automatically mark all of its child/reply feedback items as resolved too. Acceptance criteria:
1. When a parent FeedbackItem status is set to 'resolved', the backend iterates all FeedbackItems whose parent_id matches the resolved item's id and transitions their status to 'resolved' as well.
2. Cascading is recursive — if a reply itself has replies, those are also resolved.
3. The frontend Evaluate page reflects the cascaded resolution in real-time (via existing WebSocket relay).
4. If a child was already resolved, it remains resolved (no-op).
5. Resolving a child independently does NOT resolve the parent or siblings.
6. Add unit tests covering: single-level cascade, multi-level recursive cascade, already-resolved children, and independent child resolution.

comments.author: Todd Medema
comments.text:

Worktree contains no implementation code — cannot review or run tests

Issues to address:
- The worktree at opensprint.dev-7p4.5 contains only .opensprint/ metadata (prompt, context, config). No package.json, no TypeScript/JavaScript source files, and no .git directory.
- git diff main...opensprint/opensprint.dev-7p4.5 fails with 'Not a git repository' — the worktree is not a valid git checkout.
- npm test cannot be run — no package.json or test suite exists in the worktree.
- Sibling worktrees (e.g., opensprint.dev-8fek.3) contain the full codebase; this worktree appears to have been created without the implementation or was never populated.

Notes: The orchestrator should verify that the coding agent's implementation was committed to branch opensprint/opensprint.dev-7p4.5 and that the review worktree is correctly checked out from that branch. Until the worktree contains the implementation, scope compliance and code quality cannot be assessed.

title: Fix broken backend tests by correcting implementation code
description: Audit all failing backend test suites, identify root causes of failures, and fix the underlying application code (not the tests) to make them pass. Acceptance criteria: (1) All previously failing backend tests pass. (2) No tests are deleted or skipped to achieve a green suite. (3) Code changes are limited to fixing the actual defects the tests expose. (4) Any tests that were previously deleted to hide failures are restored if identifiable from git history.

title: Code quality: reduce duplication, fix tests, improve coverage and standards
Change default Human-in-the-Loop preferences to Automated
description:
Update the Project Setup Wizard's Human-in-the-Loop configuration step so that all three decision categories (Scope Changes, Architecture Decisions, Dependency Modifications) default to 'Automated' instead of the current defaults where Scope Changes and Architecture Decisions default to 'Requires approval'. Acceptance criteria:
- When a user reaches the HIL preferences step of the setup wizard, all three category toggles default to 'Automated'
- Users can still change any category to 'Notify and proceed' or 'Requires approval'
- Existing projects are not affected; only new project defaults change
- Update any seed/default config in ProjectSettings initialization to reflect the new defaults

Auto-scroll live agent output with scroll-aware toggling
description:
In the Execute phase sidebar's live agent output container, implement smart auto-scrolling behavior:

1. **Default auto-scroll:** When new agent output lines arrive, the container automatically scrolls to the bottom so the user always sees the most recent output.
2. **Disable on scroll-up:** If the user manually scrolls upward (away from the bottom), auto-scrolling is suspended so they can read earlier output without being yanked back down.
3. **Re-enable on scroll-to-bottom:** When the user scrolls back down to the bottom of the container (within a small threshold, e.g. 50px), auto-scrolling re-engages and resumes following new output.

Acceptance criteria:
- New agent output causes the container to scroll to the latest message by default.
- Scrolling up disables auto-scroll; a visual indicator (e.g. a 'Jump to bottom' pill/button) appears.
- Clicking the 'Jump to bottom' indicator or manually scrolling to the bottom re-enables auto-scroll.
- Auto-scroll state resets correctly when switching between tasks or agent sessions.
- No performance degradation with large volumes of agent output (use requestAnimationFrame or similar for scroll updates).

title: Filter thinking-type JSON from live agent output
description: In the Execute phase sidebar that displays live agent output, detect streamed JSON objects where "type" is "thinking" and replace the entire JSON blob with the text "Thinking..." in the rendered output. Acceptance criteria: (1) JSON objects with `"type":"thinking"` are not displayed raw; instead a single line reading "Thinking..." is shown in their place. (2) All other agent output (non-thinking JSON, plain text, tool use, etc.) continues to render as before. (3) Detection works on streamed/chunked output, not just complete JSON objects — if a thinking blob arrives across multiple chunks, the raw JSON must never flash before being replaced.

Epic covers: ActiveAgentsService, instrumentation of all phases, API endpoint, WebSocket events, and UI component.

title: Feedback: On the execute details sidebar, the priority and state shoul…
Add project row context menu with Archive and Delete options
description:
Add a three-dot (kebab/hamburger) menu button to the right side of each project row on the homepage. Clicking it opens a dropdown with two options: Archive and Delete. Each option opens a confirmation modal:

- **Archive modal:** Text reads "This will remove the project from the UI, but not delete its data." Buttons: Cancel (primary) and Proceed (secondary).
- **Delete modal:** Text reads "This will remove the project from the UI AND delete all OpenSprint-related data from the project folder (not including beads)." Buttons: Cancel (primary) and Proceed (secondary).

Acceptance criteria:
- Three-dot icon appears on the right side of each project row, visually aligned and themed (light/dark).
- Clicking outside the dropdown closes it.
- Cancel closes the modal with no side effects.
- Proceed on Archive calls  which removes the entry from  only.
- Proceed on Delete calls  which removes the entry from  AND deletes the  directory from the project repo folder, leaving  and all other project files intact.
- After either action, the project list refreshes and the removed project no longer appears.
- Error states (e.g., folder not found, permission denied) display a toast notification.

comments.author: Todd Medema
comments.text:
Review rejected (attempt 1):

Rejected due to deleteProject atomicity bug, scope creep, and inability to verify tests pass.

Issues to address:
- deleteProject atomicity bug (packages/backend/src/services/project.service.ts): removeProject(id) is called before fs.rm(opensprintPath). If fs.rm fails (e.g., permission denied), the project is already removed from the index — orphaned state. Fix: delete .opensprint first, then remove from index.
- Scope creep: planSlice.ts adds createPlan thunk and createPlan.fulfilled/rejected handlers — unrelated to the project row context menu ticket. Remove these changes.
- Tests could not be run: workspace does not contain source code (packages/), so npm test could not be executed to confirm all tests pass.

Notes: Scope compliance otherwise looks good: three-dot menu, Archive/Delete dropdown, correct modal text and button styling, click-outside to close, error toasts, list refresh. HomeScreen tests cover happy paths and error cases. themeTokens.integration.test.tsx change (No projects yet → Projects) may be a legitimate test fix; verify it does not introduce regressions.

Notes: Core implementation (HomeScreen context menu, archive/delete modals, backend archiveProject/deleteProject, API routes, error handling, tests for the new behavior) appears correct and meets acceptance criteria. The AddPlanModal change is the blocking issue.

title: Unify Description header with Live Output and Source Feedback collapsible headers
description: The Description section header in the feedback detail view uses a different element structure and styling (different spacing, font treatment, and collapse behavior) compared to the Live Output and Source Feedback collapsible headers. Refactor the Description header to reuse the same shared collapsible-header component and CSS classes used by Live Output and Source Feedback, ensuring consistent padding, font size, weight, icon placement, and collapse/expand behavior. Acceptance criteria: (1) Description header is rendered with the identical component and class names as Live Output and Source Feedback headers; (2) Spacing above/below the Description header matches the other two; (3) Collapse/expand interaction is identical; (4) Visual regression check confirms no unintended style differences remain.

Duplicate-key protection for beads create
description: Protect all beads create-with-parent call sites from duplicate primary key (Error 1062 / UNIQUE constraint). Add createWithRetry on BeadsService; use it in PlanService, deploy-fix-epic.service, and FeedbackService. See plan: .cursor/plans or duplicate-key protection plan.

title: Add createWithRetry and duplicate-key detection to BeadsService
description: Add private isDuplicateKeyError(err); add createWithRetry(repoPath, title, options, opts?: { fallbackToStandalone?: boolean }) with retry loop and optional fallback. Use getErrorMessage and details.stderr for detection. Return BeadsIssue | null when fallback used.

comments.author: Todd Medema
comments.text:
Review rejected (attempt 1):

Full test suite does not pass: task-service cache invalidation tests fail.

Issues to address:
- task-service.test.ts:289 — 'listTasks cache is invalidated on markDone' expects beadsListAllCalls to be 2, got 1
- task-service.test.ts:310 — 'listTasks cache is invalidated on unblock' expects beadsListAllCalls to be 2, got 1

Notes: The createWithRetry implementation meets all ticket requirements and all 50 beads-service tests pass. The failing tests mock beads.close/update, which prevents beadsCache.invalidateListAll from running (it's called from beads execImpl). The mijz.1 diff only touches beads.service.ts and beads-service.test.ts, so these may be pre-existing; however, per review instructions, all tests must pass for approval. Fix: ensure markDone and unblock invalidate beadsCache when beads mutations occur, or adjust the test mocks to trigger the expected invalidation.

Show task name instead of task ID in feedback card links
description: In the Evaluate phase feedback detail card, change the linked-task display so the link text is the task title truncated to 30 characters instead of the task ID. Remove the hover tooltip entirely. Acceptance criteria: (1) Each task link in a feedback card renders the task title as the clickable text, truncated to 30 characters with an ellipsis if longer. (2) No tooltip appears on hover. (3) The link still navigates to the correct task. (4) Truncation uses a CSS/JS approach that appends '…' when the title exceeds 30 characters.

title: Fix markdown padding in planning details sidebar
description: Adjust the top and bottom padding of the markdown content area in the planning details sidebar to match the existing left/right (side) padding. Acceptance criteria: (1) Top padding of the rendered markdown block equals the side padding. (2) Bottom padding of the rendered markdown block equals the side padding. (3) Side padding remains unchanged.

title: Improve task loading performance
Validate Phase - Feedback Submission and Mapping
Collapse Pending and Mapped feedback statuses into single Pending filter in Evaluate history dropdown
description:
In the Evaluate phase feedback history dropdown, the filter currently exposes three distinct states: Pending, Mapped, and Resolved. Users find the Pending/Mapped distinction unhelpful — both represent unresolved feedback. Combine them into a single "Pending" option in the dropdown that matches FeedbackItems with status `pending` OR `mapped`. The underlying data model (FeedbackItem.status with pending/mapped/resolved) remains unchanged; this is a display-only change.

Acceptance criteria:
- The Evaluate history dropdown shows exactly two filter options: "Pending" and "Resolved".
- Selecting "Pending" shows all feedback items whose status is either `pending` or `mapped`.
- Selecting "Resolved" continues to show only `resolved` items.
- Default selection remains "Pending" (unchanged behavior).
- No changes to the FeedbackItem data model or backend API responses.

title: Remove feedback type chip and mapped plan from Execute sidebar Source Feedback details
description: In the Execute (Build) phase sidebar, when viewing Source Feedback details for a task, remove the feedback category chip (Bug, UX, Feature, Scope) and the 'Mapped plan' field from the display. These metadata fields add clutter without providing actionable context while the user is focused on execution. The feedback text itself should remain visible. Acceptance criteria: (1) The colored category chip (e.g., 'Bug', 'UX') no longer renders in the Source Feedback section of the Execute sidebar. (2) The 'Mapped plan' label and value no longer render in that section. (3) The feedback text content still displays correctly. (4) These fields remain intact in the Evaluate/Verify phase UI where they are relevant for triage.

title: Replace full-page refresh with granular feedback card update on categorization
description:
When the Analyst agent returns a categorization for a feedback item, only the affected FeedbackCard component should re-render with the new category/mapped_plan_id/proposed_tasks — not the entire Evaluate page. Acceptance criteria:
1. After categorization completes, only the specific feedback card updates (no full page reload or re-mount of the feedback list).
2. Use the WebSocket relay to push a targeted feedback-updated event containing the feedback ID and new data; the frontend subscribes and patches only the matching card's state.
3. Other feedback cards, scroll position, and any expanded/collapsed states on the page remain unchanged.
4. Verify with multiple feedback items: submitting and categorizing one does not visually disrupt others.
5. Loading/spinner indicator should be scoped to the individual card being categorized, not page-level.

Dynamic Plan button label: 'Plan Tasks' vs 'Execute'
description: Update the plan action button to dynamically reflect the plan's readiness state. Show 'Plan Tasks' when the plan has no child implementation tasks (or only the gating task). Show 'Execute' when child tasks exist and the gating task is still open. Hide or disable the button when the gating task is already closed (plan is executing). Acceptance criteria: (1) Button reads 'Plan Tasks' for plans with zero non-gate child tasks. (2) Button reads 'Execute' for plans with generated child tasks and an open gate. (3) After Execute is clicked, the gating task is closed and child tasks become eligible for bd ready. (4) Button state updates reactively when tasks are added or the gate is closed.

title: Add draggable sidebar resizing to Sketch and Execute phases
description:
Extend the drag-to-resize sidebar behavior already implemented in Plan mode to the Sketch and Execute phase layouts. Extract the existing resize handle/logic from the Plan sidebar into a shared, reusable component (e.g., ResizableSidebar or a resize hook). Apply it to:

1. **Sketch phase** — the PRD section sidebar and/or the chat panel divider.
2. **Execute phase** — the task detail sidebar and/or the Kanban side panel.

Acceptance criteria:
- Users can click-and-drag the sidebar edge to resize in both Sketch and Execute modes.
- Resize behavior matches the existing Plan mode implementation (cursor style, min/max width constraints, smooth drag tracking).
- Sidebar width preference persists across navigation within a session (in component state or localStorage).
- Resize handle is visually consistent across all three phases.
- No regressions to Plan mode resize behavior.

Include task title in all orchestrator commit messages
description: Update the orchestrator's git commit queue to include the task/issue title after the task ID in all commit messages, separated by a colon. Currently PRD §5.9 defines patterns like  and . The merge pattern already includes the title, but other commit types (beads JSONL exports, status transitions) may only reference IDs. Ensure every commit message that references a task ID also includes  immediately after the ID. Acceptance criteria: (1) All orchestrator-generated commits that reference a bead ID include the task title after the ID in the format ; (2) Beads export commits summarize which tasks changed by name, not just ID; (3) Existing commit message patterns for PRD updates and worktree merges remain consistent with the new convention; (4) Unit tests verify commit message formatting for each commit type in the queue.
